data.title: Alan Pell Crawford's book "This Fierce People" wins $50,000 American Battlefield Trust Prize. Nomination window opens for 2025 published books.
data.description: The nomination period is currently open for military history books released in 2025 to be eligible for the $50,000 prize next year. Recently, historian Alan Pell Crawford was awarded the $50,000 American Battlefield Trust Prize for History for his book "This Fierce..." in Washington on October 16, 2025.

data.title: CPSC issues warnings on HolyBase Infant Walkers, advising consumers to stop use immediately. View details at cpsc.gov.
data.description: On October 16, 2025, the Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) issued a public announcement regarding product safety concerns. The CPSC has advised consumers to discontinue the use of HolyBase Infant Walkers due to the potential risk of serious injury. For more information and to access the full list of product safety warnings and recalls, individuals can visit the official CPSC website at www.cpsc.gov. It is crucial for consumers to take immediate action in response to these safety alerts to safeguard against possible harm.

data.title: Riverside Research's VERG wins NVC Veteran Employee Resource Group of the Year Award in September 2025 for outstanding contributions.
data.description: In September 2025, Riverside Research's Veterans' Employee Resource Group (VERG) was honored with the Veteran Employee Resource Group of the Year Award by the Northern Virginia Chamber (NVC) in Washington. The award acknowledges outstanding achievement by a Veteran Employee Resource Group and highlights their contributions to the community. This recognition underscores VERG's commitment to supporting veterans' initiatives and fostering a culture of inclusivity within the organization.

data.title: Bentley Systems announces Bentley Infrastructure Cloud Connect to enhance collaboration for infrastructure experts.
data.description: Amsterdam - Bentley Systems, Incorporated (Nasdaq: BSY), an infrastructure engineering software provider, announced today the launch of Bentley Infrastructure Cloud Connect as a new foundational layer for the Bentley Infrastructure Cloud in the Year in Infrastructure 2025 event. Connect aims to provide infrastructure professionals with a connected data environment and integrated experience for handling project and asset data, enhancing collaboration across the infrastructure lifecycle and value chain. Bentley Systems' Chief Technology Officer, Julien Moutte, stated, "Infrastructure data exists in various forms such as models, PDFs, inspection forms, photos, IoT sensors, but they are rarely connected."

data.title: Op-Ed criticizes Colorado PDAB for prioritizing politics over patent access, risking patient access to life-saving drugs.
data.description: An opinion piece published in Washington on October 16, 2025, shed light on the failings of the CO PDAB, emphasizing the concerning implications of upper payment limits (UPL) on patient access to vital medications. The article, attributed to the Rare Access Action Project (RAAP), highlighted the risks associated with UPL, stressing the potential threats it poses to patients in need of life-saving drugs. This insight underscores the critical nature of addressing UPL-related issues within the healthcare system to safeguard patient well-being.

data.title: Majority of Gen Z workers use ChatGPT to slack off at work; 1 in 3 confide in AI; nearly half say it knows them better.
data.description: One out of every three individuals have shared information with artificial intelligence that they have never disclosed to a person, while almost 50% believe that ChatGPT understands them more effectively than their supervisor. The data was obtained from a recent survey conducted in October 2025 by Resume.org, a prominent resume-building platform, which involved 1,000 participants.

data.title: Nabsys shows at ASHG 2025 that 70% of IC/BPS patients have psych comorbidities, with novel genetic insights using EGM.
data.description: A recent multi-omics research using electronic genome mapping (EGM) has found that 70% of individuals diagnosed with interstitial cystitis/bladder pain syndrome (IC/BPS) also have psychiatric comorbidities. This study has provided new proteomic and genetic discoveries into IC/BPS. EGM has proven to offer efficient analysis and expandable capabilities for further research in this field.

data.title: Global anti-Parkinson's drugs market set to reach $9.2B by 2030, growing from $6.1B in 2025, at 8.6% CAGR. Rising prevalence fuels demand for advanced treatments. Pharmaceutical initiatives, investments, and a robust pipeline are driving market growth.
data.description: The global market for anti-Parkinson's drugs is expected to increase from $6.1 billion in 2025 to $9.2 billion by 2030, with a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 8.6%. The market is segmented by drug class, such as dopaminergic agents, dopamine agonists, COMT inhibitors, MAO-B inhibitors, anticholinergics, and others, across regions like North America, Europe, Asia-Pacific, the Middle East and Africa, and South America. Factors driving this growth include the growing prevalence of Parkinson's disease, high unmet medical needs for effective treatments, strategic initiatives by pharmaceutical companies, and public and private investments in research and development. The market's expansion also benefits from a robust pipeline of innovative therapies like gene therapies and RNA-based drugs. This trend signifies a promising outlook for stakeholders involved in anti-Parkinson's drug development.

data.title: Colorado's fastest-growing electrical contractor CEO chose Copperweld® Building Wire for his new home for its reliability.
data.description: In Brentwood, Tennessee, on October 16, 2025, Ryan McHugh, the founder and CEO of Hi Power Electric, emphasized the importance of reliability in his work. When wiring his own home, he opted for the same product that contributed to his company's success.

data.title: MTN Digital Infrastructure partners with Seekr to boost AI adoption in Africa, signing MOU on integrating AI into its Data Centre ecosystem.
data.description: MTN Digital Infrastructure has recently entered into a Memorandum of Understanding with Seekr to jointly work on incorporating advanced AI technologies into its Data Centre network. The collaboration involves Seekr Technologies, a prominent US-based AI firm, and signals a significant step towards enhancing the efficiency and capabilities of MTN's operations. This partnership, announced on October 16, 2025, in Reston, Virginia, and Dubai, United Arab Emirates, underlines a strategic move by MTN to bring cutting-edge technology into its infrastructure. This agreement marks a pivotal moment for both companies as they aim to leverage the power of artificial intelligence in the digital landscape.
